content:
Weekly Review: June 10-17, 2026

💚 Zyn-Free Streak

12 days clean. That's real. The first two weeks are genuinely the hardest part, and you're through them. Every shift you get through without reaching for a pouch is proof that you're stronger than the habit. Keep going.

😴 Sleep and Recovery

This is the area that needs the most attention right now. You averaged 5.9 hours of sleep and a sleep score of 66, which is below where you want to be consistently. The good news is that your body battery at wake was an impressive 96 on average, and your resting heart rate of 44 is excellent. Your HRV at 72.5 is solid too. Your stress score of 22 is low, which tells me the issue isn't anxiety or overtraining - it's simply not enough time in bed, likely down to shift scheduling. Your training readiness averaging 43.3 reflects that sleep debt directly. You're not broken, you're just short on hours. Where you can, protect sleep like a training session.

🏃 Training

Two runs totalling 14.7km with an average pace of 6:28 per km. You also got a 5km walk in. Your weekly intensity minutes came in at 138, which is close to your 150 goal but just short. The main thing I want to flag is your average running heart rate of 164 bpm. That puts you right in Z3 territory, edging toward Z4. With your sleep and readiness scores where they are this week, running harder than necessary is borrowing from a recovery account that isn't fully topped up. Try to bring at least one of your weekly runs down into Z2, keeping HR under 145. It will feel embarrassingly slow at first, but it builds your aerobic base without adding recovery cost. Two runs is a reasonable week given the shifts and the sleep situation. No bikes this week, which is fine.

🍽 Nutrition

Calories were almost perfect at 1612 against a target of 1600. Carbs at 133g were slightly under the 145g target, and fat at 52g was essentially on point at 51g target. The gap to close is protein. You averaged 110g against a 150g target, which is a 40g daily shortfall. Over six logged days that's a significant cumulative gap when your goal is body recomposition. Protein is the one macro that directly protects your muscle while in a deficit, so this is worth prioritising. Think about where you can add 30 to 40g more per day without blowing calories - Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, an extra chicken breast, a protein shake. You logged 6 out of 7 days which is good consistency. Keep that up.

No weight or body fat data this week, which makes it harder to track progress toward your 65kg goal. Even a once-weekly weigh-in first thing in the morning gives us useful information over time.

📊 The Honest Summary

You're building something real here. The Zyn quit is sticking, your training is consistent, and your calories are dialled in. The two things to focus on going into next week are getting protein closer to 150g daily and protecting whatever sleep you can. Everything else follows from those two things. Good week, Jørgen. Keep the thread going.
